node.plaintext:
We are very pleased to announce that we now have an Instagram account! You can
find us at @particl.official or simply click here
[https://www.instagram.com/particl.official/] to view our feed. Instagram is a
social network that’s getting increasingly popular with crypto users and will
hopefully help us reach new people and push a different style of content. Follow
our page and #BePART [https://paper.dropbox.com/?q=%23BePART] of this journey
with us!

Don’t forget to also follow our other social media accounts on Facebook
[https://www.facebook.com/ParticlProject/], Twitter
[https://twitter.com/ParticlProject], and LinkedIn
[https://www.linkedin.com/company/particl/].

node.plaintext:
We are only 6 days away until Particl Project’s decentralized marketplace ALPHA
launches for everyone. #BePART of an online shopping revolution!

Testing has wrapped up
Thank you for all the community testers helping on non-official ALPHA builds the
past 6+ weeks. Both of our Open Dev
[https://riot.im/app/#/room/#particl-dev:matrix.org] and Testing
[https://riot.im/app/#/room/#particl-testing:matrix.org] channels have been busy
with community and team developers, test-vendors and test-customers working on
nearly daily builds across multiple operating systems as we meticulously
corrected bugs/issues.

These days of troubleshooting and problem solving were crucial to getting a
properly working MVP of our decentralized marketplace —which translates into
signed-off official builds of ALPHA. 👍

Status Report 📃
As of yesterday the status of the marketplace is the following:

 * Build 1016/7 are being tested and proven to be very stable.
 * Issue reported with decimals not showing up in checkout process —Fixed and
   merged
 * Issue reported where multiple items in checkout caused problems when placing
   the order —Fix currently being tested

The time has come!
I’m excited to announce that next Thursday, May 31st, Particl will be releasing
our first Marketplace ALPHA* builds on our Github page. As with all our our
work, our code and interfaces are all open-source and publicly audit-able.

*ALPHA is for Testnet only.

With less than a week and counting, I encourage everyone to keep plugged in and
be part of this huge step for the Particl project. You can find links to all our
social media and community chat channels here
[https://particl.wiki/socialmedia-channels].

More things in the wings
Work never stops when building the future my friends.

I’ve linked to our testing and development channels above and our community
channels as well. Please stay connected, get involved
[https://particl.news/the-partaker-hub-81db23d501] and help us further along
this first milestone of our decentralized marketplace.

As this is built on our test network, users can expect new updates that come
along to fix bugs found in the shopping experience, security patches needed to
protect us, governance features to begin showing up and privacy enhancements to
be regularly added.

Next week we will all have our hands on the first build of a private,
decentralized marketplace aimed at disrupting the way people buy and sell online 
anywhere in the world.

Download, create a store, post some items for sale and #BePART of a revolution.

Thank You
Particl Team

node.title: Particl Development Roadmap Update

primary_tag.name: Development Updates
primary_tag.description: See a blockchain-powered autonomous internet marketplace wunderkind grow. You make a difference with Particl.

tags.name: Development Updates
tags.description: See a blockchain-powered autonomous internet marketplace wunderkind grow. You make a difference with Particl.

node.plaintext:
Roadmap v2.0
We teased this a bit in our last AMA livestream [https://youtu.be/7DNm9l3GmRs],
and here is a more complete look at what the Particl development team will be
working towards for the upcoming 6 months and beyond. 
https://particl.io/roadmap/

The following items are all in our 2018–2019 pipeline. We’ve identified
important items that are focus features over the next 6 months and beyond but
aren’t tying these to quarters as we did in our Roadmap v1.2
[https://particl.news/project-update-november-8th-2017-c0437292e1f8].

*New items can be added or taken away at anytime, this is a living document.

IN PROGRESS:
With the release
[https://particl.news/release-particl-core-0-16-0-1-1dad242b075] of Particl Core
0.16.0.1 the development team debuts a couple features that they have been
working on.

Support for Hardware Signing Devices
Particl Core 0.16 introduces generic support for Hardware Signing Devices to
provide key-signing capabilities.

Ledger Integration
Initial support for the Ledger Hardware Wallet is here!

This has not yet been extended to blind or anon tranactions and therefore
currently cannot be used exclusively for all functions of the wallet. Support
will continue to be extended in future versions.

Stealth Addresses V2
In order to facilitate deterministic generation of stealth addresses between
Particl Core and Signing Devices the method used to generate these addresses had
to be changed.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Particl Marketplace (MP)
The majority of our team will be focused on the private decentralized
marketplace over the next 6 months. We are readying our Alpha release of the
Particl Marketplace as I write this.

ALPHA is the long-awaited decentralized marketplace dApp within Particl Desktop
[https://particl.io/downloads/]. The Alpha release of the marketplace will be
barebones to start as users begin to test and progress through the
buying/selling process.

As more users onboard we’ll continue refining the user experience and the tools
available to vendors and shoppers.

Listing Governance
Governance won’t be in our initial release of the Markeplace but is one of the
items in the pipeline this year. The vision of the Particl Project is to be a
privacy focused platform for decentralized applications.

The Particl Marketplace is being built to be self-sustaining, self-governing and
completely decentralized. Governance is a huge piece of the puzzle that the team
will be focused on developing to protect privacy and protect users.

Software Development Kit (SDK)
A plan to have a SDK for developers is in the pipeline. The SDK will allow the
creation of applications within the Marketplace or Desktop.

This might not come in 2018 from the Particl team (it is a pipeline item in the
future) but could be a community development item someone takes on as a side
project to create. The full support of the dev team will be available to anyone
wanting to contribute and help speed of delivery of valuable items.

> A software development kit is typically a set of software development tools that
allows the creation of applications for a certain software package, software
framework, hardware platform, computer system, video game console, operating
system, or similar development platform. — Wikipedia
ZMQ for MP Messages
Short for ZeroMQ [http://zguide.zeromq.org/page:all#-MQ-in-a-Hundred-Words],
this is an automated messaging system that allows messages and notifications to
be automatically pushed to Particl Desktop. An example would be if Particl
Desktop has an update available, ZMQ will push notification to Particl Desktop
and users will see a notice a new version of the Desktop is available.

BETA Marketplace
Our next major release of the decentralized marketplace will be BETA.

Beta MP may or may-not be a mainnet viable product. It is too early to tell but
is something the team will be transparent in communicating.

Factors such as Governance and privacy (RingCT) are big factors on whether a
mainnet marketplace is available in the next 6 months on mainnet. 50/50 is the
best bet at the moment; being completely honest and forthcoming.

A Beta marketplace release with governance but without RingCT could happen with
our Confidential TX’s (CT) already providing privacy on mainnet. CT do a good
job of hiding amounts (MAD escrow) but don’t provide complete privacy for
addresses of vendors and buyers in a escrow contract.

Privacy and Governance are 2 of the major factors that ultimately will determine
the BETA marketplace release in the coming 6 months.

Full Particl Marketplace Release
All versions of the MP leading up to full release will be testing versions and
live versions with new features introduced at each release. Once RingCT is on
mainnet, Governance is fully developed, and currency-agnostic options like
cross-chain atomic swaps/integrations have been through the proper testing
environment we’ll plan a Full Particl Marketplace Release.

This will be the truly private decentralized marketplace that will disrupt the
way you buy and sell online.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Ring Confidential Transactions (RingCT)
Speaking of privacy and RingCT, we will see some exciting movement on this
privacy enhancement in the next 6 months.

Academic Review
If you aren’t aware, NJIT research Qiang Tang [https://web.njit.edu/~qiang/] is
leading a group in a review of our RingCT privacy on Particl Testnet. Particl
Foundation created a grant with the university in Sept. 2017 for this research,
Qiang’s lab received the grant money in Nov. 2017
[https://web.njit.edu/~qiang/#activities] and began working on a 6 month review
in Dec. 2017.

They are 3 months into review and will provide the foundation with a report
after 6 months on the privacy, cryptography and security recommendations for
Particl’s RingCT implementation on the latest Bitcoin codebase.

Bulletproofs: Short Proofs for Confidential Transactions
A very exciting paper [https://eprint.iacr.org/2017/1066.pdf] was released in
the middle of our grant with Qiang (NJIT) that significantly reduces the burden
of confidential transactions on the blockchain.

> Bulletproofs greatly improve on the linear (in n) sized range proofs in existing
proposals for confidential transactions in Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies.
Particl is the only Bitcoin-based cryptocurrency with Confidential Transactions
live and available today. We don’t allow CT to be default because of the
size/cost this privacy introduces to transactions. Here is an infographic you
may have seen showing the size/cost of each level of privacy for PART.

You can see that transaction size of CT and RingCT are 20x that of the
pseudo-anonymous “public” state most users default to.

Bulletproofs is a big development that we will be reviewing this year. We
estimate research, development, implementation and testing will be a 3–4 month
part-time assignment for Tecnovert, Particl Lead Core Developer.

RingCT — Mainnet
It is fair to give a realistic checklist of the steps needed to see RingCT on
Particl Mainnet in the coming months. Some work has begun, some work will need
to be adjusted and some work has yet to begin.

*Future dates are estimates and subject to change.

 * June 2017
   [https://particl.news/particl-dev-update-june-1st-2017-f736948a338d]— Particl
   is the first Bitcoin-based cryptocurrency with CT and RingCT on pre-launch
   testnets
 * July 2017
   [https://particl.news/particl-launch-update-july-17th-2017-15ec6d4fd6a8]
   — Particl launches as first project with privacy available through
   Confidential Transactions on mainnet. RingCT continues on new versions of
   testnet
 * September 2017— Particl Foundation creates a research grant with the New
   Jersey Institute of Technology to review our RingCT implementation on Bitcoin
   codebase.
 * November 2017— Qiang Tang’s research lab receives the Particl Foundation
   grant
 * December 2017— Research begins on Particl RingCT
 * *est. April 2018 — Particl begins developing Bulletproofs on testnet. Working
   versions will be relayed to NJIT to bootstrap onto their ongoing research
   efforts.
 * *est. June 2018 — NJIT researchers present Particl Foundation with their
   review and recommendation of our RingCT / CT / Bulletproofs development on
   Bitcoin codebase.
 * *est. July 2018 — Particl development will modify/improve CT/RingCT code
   based on NJIT recommendations. First Bulletproofs on Testnet
 * *est. August 2018 — Particl Foundation will have improved code reviewed (1
   month peer review, 3rd party)
 * *est. September 2018 — Particl development will make any changes necessary.
   New code will be on Testnet. Hardfork announced for RingCT to be moved to
   Mainnet
 * *est. October 2018 — Hardfork completed, RingCT on Mainnet alongside
   Bulletproofs CT


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

PIPELINE:
Copay Cold Staking
Particl Copay is a secure wallet made for desktop and mobile OS. We plan to
integrate Particl Cold Staking into this multi-signature wallet.

Community Fund Vote
There is PART remaining from the Community Initiative Fund #1
[https://particl.news/a-community-driven-initiative-e26724100c3a]— roughly
30,000 PART (at this time) with a few more claims still being vetted. The
community needs to create a plan for these extra coins that were donated but not
needed. Start brainstorming!

Lightning Network
A lot of excitement is stirring in the space because of lightning (LND)
releasing on mainnet. Obviously, this is a big item on our radar as we build
paths for other cryptocurrency coins to be used on Particl Marketplace.

#BlindLightning | #BlackLightning
What if Particl Confidential TXs or even RingCT could be leveraged in atomic
swaps/ lightning network transactions between PART and other coins — providing
true privacy for non-private coins?


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Particl Desktop — future versions
In our last AMAFAQ livestream we also mentioned the current status of our
flagship app, Particl Desktop.

New features related to the Particl Marketplace integration into Desktop will be
ongoing throughout 2018. We’ve had a lot of requests for extra widgets and
features from the community/team that are moving down the pipeline.

Below we’ve begun a checklist of requested features.

 * Multiwallet modal
 * Coin Control
 * Alias Addresses
 * Balances and Staking graphs
 * Remote Daemon Connection
 * Tor Integration
 * Settings modal
 * Modal Refactoring

Particl Marketplace is the main focus of the team moving forward, but you can
expect to see some of these features coming into Desktop as well. The
marketplace is priority number one.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We’re excited to see the impact Particl Marketplace and PART have in our space
for 2018–2019. We all see an opportunity to provide use cases for other coins
with our privacy focused decentralized applications — starting with the
Marketplace.

Join us in our Riot/Telegram channels to get involved:

Riot [https://riot.im/app/#/room/#particl:matrix.org] | Telegram
[https://t.me/particlproject] | Discord [https://discordapp.com/invite/2tVJaZ9]

Thank You
Particl Team

There is PART remaining from the Community Initiative Fund #1 — roughly 30,000 PART (at this time) with a few more claims still being vetted. The community needs to create a plan for these extra coins that were donated but not needed. Start brainstorming!

Lightning Network

A lot of excitement is stirring in the space because of lightning (LND) releasing on mainnet. Obviously, this is a big item on our radar as we build paths for other cryptocurrency coins to be used on Particl Marketplace.

#BlindLightning | #BlackLightning

What if Particl Confidential TXs or even RingCT could be leveraged in atomic swaps/ lightning network transactions between PART and other coins — providing true privacy for non-private coins?

Particl Desktop — future versions

In our last AMAFAQ livestream we also mentioned the current status of our flagship app, Particl Desktop.

New features related to the Particl Marketplace integration into Desktop will be ongoing throughout 2018. We’ve had a lot of requests for extra widgets and features from the community/team that are moving down the pipeline.

Below we’ve begun a checklist of requested features.

  • Multiwallet modal
  • Coin Control
  • Alias Addresses
  • Balances and Staking graphs
  • Remote Daemon Connection
  • Tor Integration
  • Settings modal
  • Modal Refactoring

Particl Marketplace is the main focus of the team moving forward, but you can expect to see some of these features coming into Desktop as well. The marketplace is priority number one.

We’re excited to see the impact Particl Marketplace and PART have in our space for 2018–2019. We all see an opportunity to provide use cases for other coins with our privacy focused decentralized applications — starting with the Marketplace.

Join us in our Riot/Telegram channels to get involved:

Riot | Telegram | Discord

Thank You

Particl Team

Roadmap v2.0

We teased this a bit in our last AMA livestream, and here is a more complete look at what the Particl development team will be working towards for the upcoming 6 months and beyond. https://particl.io/roadmap/

The following items are all in our 2018–2019 pipeline. We’ve identified important items that are focus features over the next 6 months and beyond but aren’t tying these to quarters as we did in our Roadmap v1.2.

*New items can be added or taken away at anytime, this is a living document.

IN PROGRESS:

With the release of Particl Core 0.16.0.1 the development team debuts a couple features that they have been working on.

Support for Hardware Signing Devices

Particl Core 0.16 introduces generic support for Hardware Signing Devices to provide key-signing capabilities.

Ledger Integration

Initial support for the Ledger Hardware Wallet is here!

This has not yet been extended to blind or anon tranactions and therefore currently cannot be used exclusively for all functions of the wallet. Support will continue to be extended in future versions.

Stealth Addresses V2

In order to facilitate deterministic generation of stealth addresses between Particl Core and Signing Devices the method used to generate these addresses had to be changed.

Particl Marketplace (MP)

The majority of our team will be focused on the private decentralized marketplace over the next 6 months. We are readying our Alpha release of the Particl Marketplace as I write this.

ALPHA is the long-awaited decentralized marketplace dApp within Particl Desktop. The Alpha release of the marketplace will be barebones to start as users begin to test and progress through the buying/selling process.

As more users onboard we’ll continue refining the user experience and the tools available to vendors and shoppers.

Listing Governance

Governance won’t be in our initial release of the Markeplace but is one of the items in the pipeline this year. The vision of the Particl Project is to be a privacy focused platform for decentralized applications.

The Particl Marketplace is being built to be self-sustaining, self-governing and completely decentralized. Governance is a huge piece of the puzzle that the team will be focused on developing to protect privacy and protect users.

Software Development Kit (SDK)

A plan to have a SDK for developers is in the pipeline. The SDK will allow the creation of applications within the Marketplace or Desktop.

This might not come in 2018 from the Particl team (it is a pipeline item in the future) but could be a community development item someone takes on as a side project to create. The full support of the dev team will be available to anyone wanting to contribute and help speed of delivery of valuable items.

A software development kit is typically a set of software development tools that allows the creation of applications for a certain software package, software framework, hardware platform, computer system, video game console, operating system, or similar development platform. — Wikipedia

ZMQ for MP Messages

Short for ZeroMQ, this is an automated messaging system that allows messages and notifications to be automatically pushed to Particl Desktop. An example would be if Particl Desktop has an update available, ZMQ will push notification to Particl Desktop and users will see a notice a new version of the Desktop is available.

BETA Marketplace

Our next major release of the decentralized marketplace will be BETA.

Beta MP may or may-not be a mainnet viable product. It is too early to tell but is something the team will be transparent in communicating.

Factors such as Governance and privacy (RingCT) are big factors on whether a mainnet marketplace is available in the next 6 months on mainnet. 50/50 is the best bet at the moment; being completely honest and forthcoming.

A Beta marketplace release with governance but without RingCT could happen with our Confidential TX’s (CT) already providing privacy on mainnet. CT do a good job of hiding amounts (MAD escrow) but don’t provide complete privacy for addresses of vendors and buyers in a escrow contract.

Privacy and Governance are 2 of the major factors that ultimately will determine the BETA marketplace release in the coming 6 months.

Full Particl Marketplace Release

All versions of the MP leading up to full release will be testing versions and live versions with new features introduced at each release. Once RingCT is on mainnet, Governance is fully developed, and currency-agnostic options like cross-chain atomic swaps/integrations have been through the proper testing environment we’ll plan a Full Particl Marketplace Release.

This will be the truly private decentralized marketplace that will disrupt the way you buy and sell online.

Ring Confidential Transactions (RingCT)

Speaking of privacy and RingCT, we will see some exciting movement on this privacy enhancement in the next 6 months.

Academic Review

If you aren’t aware, NJIT research Qiang Tang is leading a group in a review of our RingCT privacy on Particl Testnet. Particl Foundation created a grant with the university in Sept. 2017 for this research, Qiang’s lab received the grant money in Nov. 2017 and began working on a 6 month review in Dec. 2017.

They are 3 months into review and will provide the foundation with a report after 6 months on the privacy, cryptography and security recommendations for Particl’s RingCT implementation on the latest Bitcoin codebase.

Bulletproofs: Short Proofs for Confidential Transactions

A very exciting paper was released in the middle of our grant with Qiang (NJIT) that significantly reduces the burden of confidential transactions on the blockchain.

Bulletproofs greatly improve on the linear (in n) sized range proofs in existing proposals for confidential transactions in Bitcoin and other cryptocurrencies.

Particl is the only Bitcoin-based cryptocurrency with Confidential Transactions live and available today. We don’t allow CT to be default because of the size/cost this privacy introduces to transactions. Here is an infographic you may have seen showing the size/cost of each level of privacy for PART.

You can see that transaction size of CT and RingCT are 20x that of the pseudo-anonymous “public” state most users default to.

Bulletproofs is a big development that we will be reviewing this year. We estimate research, development, implementation and testing will be a 3–4 month part-time assignment for Tecnovert, Particl Lead Core Developer.

RingCT — Mainnet

It is fair to give a realistic checklist of the steps needed to see RingCT on Particl Mainnet in the coming months. Some work has begun, some work will need to be adjusted and some work has yet to begin.

*Future dates are estimates and subject to change.

  • June 2017 — Particl is the first Bitcoin-based cryptocurrency with CT and RingCT on pre-launch testnets
  • July 2017 — Particl launches as first project with privacy available through Confidential Transactions on mainnet. RingCT continues on new versions of testnet
  • September 2017 — Particl Foundation creates a research grant with the New Jersey Institute of Technology to review our RingCT implementation on Bitcoin codebase.
  • November 2017 — Qiang Tang’s research lab receives the Particl Foundation grant
  • December 2017 — Research begins on Particl RingCT
  • *est. April 2018 — Particl begins developing Bulletproofs on testnet. Working versions will be relayed to NJIT to bootstrap onto their ongoing research efforts.
  • *est. June 2018 — NJIT researchers present Particl Foundation with their review and recommendation of our RingCT / CT / Bulletproofs development on Bitcoin codebase.
  • *est. July 2018 — Particl development will modify/improve CT/RingCT code based on NJIT recommendations. First Bulletproofs on Testnet
  • *est. August 2018 — Particl Foundation will have improved code reviewed (1 month peer review, 3rd party)
  • *est. September 2018 — Particl development will make any changes necessary. New code will be on Testnet. Hardfork announced for RingCT to be moved to Mainnet
  • *est. October 2018 — Hardfork completed, RingCT on Mainnet alongside Bulletproofs CT

PIPELINE:

Copay Cold Staking

Particl Copay is a secure wallet made for desktop and mobile OS. We plan to integrate Particl Cold Staking into this multi-signature wallet.

Below we’ve begun a checklist of requested features.

  • Multiwallet modal

  • Balances and Staking graphs
  • Remote Daemon Connection
  • Tor Integration
  • Settings modal
  • Modal Refactoring

Particl Marketplace is the main focus of the team moving forward, but you can expect to see some of these features coming into Desktop as well. The marketplace is priority number one.

We’re excited to see the impact Particl Marketplace and PART have in our space for 2018–2019. We all see an opportunity to provide use cases for other coins with our privacy focused decentralized applications — starting with the Marketplace.

Particl Team

node.plaintext:
Ledger Support and Stealth Addresses v2
This is a new major version release, including new features, various bug fixes
and performance improvements, as well as updated translations.

How to Upgrade
If you are running an older version, shut it down. Wait until it has completely
shut down (which might take a few minutes for older versions), then run the
installer (on Windows) or just copy over /Applications/Particl-Qt (on Mac) or
particld/ particl-qt (on Linux).

Compatibility
Particl Core is extensively tested on multiple operating systems using the Linux
kernel, macOS 10.8+, and Windows Vista and later. Windows XP is not supported.

particl/particl-core
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.16.0.1]
particl-core - Particl Core integration/staging tree
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.16.0.1]github.com
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.16.0.1]

*Particl Core should also work on most other Unix-like systems but is not
frequently tested on them.

Notable changes — Wallet
Support for Hardware Signing Devices
Particl Core 0.16 introduces generic support for Hardware Signing Devices to
provide key-signing capabilities.

 * New RPC commands
   * listdevices
   * getdeviceinfo
   * initaccountfromdevice
   * getdevicepublickey
   * getdevicexpub
   * devicegetnewstealthaddress
   * devicesignmessage`
   * devicesignrawtransaction

Ledger Hardware Support
Initial support for the Ledger Hardware Wallet has been included.
This has not yet been extended to blind or anon tranactions and therefore
currently cannot be used exclusively for all functions of the wallet. Support
will continue to be extended in future versions.

*Note, this is still considered “expert” mode for those who have a good
understanding of how the Protocol works under the hood and are comfortable
compiling and flashing developer tools on a Ledger device.

Stealth Address v2
In order to facilitate deterministic generation of stealth addresses between
Particl Core and Signing Devices the method used to generate these addresses has
been changed.

 * New RPC command devicegetnewstealthaddress will generate a v2 Stealth Address
   from the Hardware Signing Device.
 * Updated RPC command getnewstealthaddress now supports a new boolean makeV2
   (default false) which will generate a v2 Stealth Address from the same method
   used for hardware wallets. This will be updated in a future release to
   default to true for all new addresses generated.

*Note, Legacy Stealth Addresses are not supported on Hardware Signing Devices.
If you have PART/tPART received on the Legacy Stealth Addresses then we suggest
you send those PART/tPART to a non-Stealth Address before attempting to use with
the Hardware Signing Devices.

> PART = coins on mainnet | tPART* = coins on testnet*tPART have (0)zero value

Replace-By-Fee by default in GUI
The send screen now uses BIP125 RBF by default, regardless of -walletrbf. There
is a checkbox to mark the transaction as final.

The RPC default remains unchanged: to use RBF, launch with -walletrbf=1 or use
the replaceable argument for individual transactions.

Wallets directory configuration (-walletdir)
Particl Core now has more flexibility in where the wallets directory can be
located. Previously wallet database files were stored at the top level of the
particl data directory. The behavior is now:

 * For new installations (where the data directory doesn’t already exist),
   wallets will now be stored in a new wallets/ subdirectory inside the data
   directory by default.
 * For existing nodes (where the data directory already exists), wallets will be
   stored in the data directory root by default. If a wallets/ subdirectory
   already exists in the data directory root, then wallets will be stored in the
   wallets/ subdirectory by default.
 * The location of the wallets directory can be overridden by specifying a
   -walletdir=<path> option where <path> can be an absolute path to a
   directory or directory symlink.

*Care should be taken when choosing the wallets directory location, as if it
becomes unavailable during operation, funds may be lost.

Official Particl 0.16 Release Notes:
For an extensive list of all changes in this release please review the 0.16
release notes here: 
https://github.com/particl/particl-core/blob/master/doc/release-notes/release-notes-0.16.0.1.md

Thank You
Particl Team

The RPC default remains unchanged: to use RBF, launch with -walletrbf=1 or use the replaceable argument for individual transactions.

Wallets directory configuration (-walletdir)

Particl Core now has more flexibility in where the wallets directory can be located. Previously wallet database files were stored at the top level of the particl data directory. The behavior is now:

  • For new installations (where the data directory doesn’t already exist), wallets will now be stored in a new wallets/ subdirectory inside the data directory by default.
  • For existing nodes (where the data directory already exists), wallets will be stored in the data directory root by default. If a wallets/ subdirectory already exists in the data directory root, then wallets will be stored in the wallets/ subdirectory by default.
  • The location of the wallets directory can be overridden by specifying a
    -walletdir=<path> option where <path> can be an absolute path to a
    directory or directory symlink.

*Care should be taken when choosing the wallets directory location, as if it becomes unavailable during operation, funds may be lost.

Official Particl 0.16 Release Notes:

For an extensive list of all changes in this release please review the 0.16 release notes here: https://github.com/particl/particl-core/blob/master/doc/release-notes/release-notes-0.16.0.1.md

Thank You

Particl Team

node.plaintext:
A few months back, Joe Fisher — one of Particl’s advisors — reached out to
YouTuber Desi-Rae Campbell to do a series of videos on the Particl Project. His
goal was to tap into the video blog genre to help promote Particl, as well as
privacy rights, decentralized ecommerce and financial freedom (individual
sovereignty).

We are excited to bring Desi-Rae on to exclusively cover Particl and also
support her research into other benefits blockchain tech and the space can lead
us in the future.

I had a chance to ask her some questions about her background and passions
recently.

PS: You have an academic process by how you learn and then present your content.
What is your background?

DRC: My background is in scientific research and data collection, as well as
visual art, specifically oil painting. I studied Biology and Studio Arts in
school and have always been interested in many things and the process of
learning itself. As you can possibly tell from my videos, I enjoy encountering
something new and then finding out more about it, particularly by doing research
that provides data to answer a question. But I am also interested in the
aesthetic environment of a presentation, in terms of visuals, tone and mood, and
not only the presentation of the actual information.

PS: How did you get into the cryptocurrency space?

DRC: I got into the cryptocurrency space through osmosis. I knew and followed
people who were interested in cryptocurrency. I had a minimal amount of interest
in the technology behind it. This is in the sense that I like to understand how
the world around me works, and not be ignorant of the basic functioning of many
things that I come to rely upon and use. However, I may not know how to build
something myself. I was also drawn into the cryptocurrency space through the
appeal of investing long term for financial gain, something which is in some
ways barred from individuals who are not already in the financial world due to
how the law handles those kinds of scenarios.

PS: What type of content do you want to bring to the Particl community and the
cryptocurrency space in general?

DRC: I want to bring content to the cryptocurrency space that makes people
curious, engaged, and hopefully enthusiastic about cryptocurrency. There is a
lot of content available for anyone who wants to learn. Really, there are video
series and lectures, from any standpoint, such as knowing how to code,
understanding how blockchains or features of a cryptocurrency work, figuring out
how to trade or invest, and so on. Anything. Anyone can learn anything. So, my
interest is in shaping how a person can interact within that space, with the
personalities involved and the way they receive that information. And I think my
bigger focus would be on understanding concepts rather than the full details,
and just having a line of communication between the official project and the
community at large or potential supporters or contributors.

PS: Do you think privacy is important?

DRC: With an emphatic yes, I think that privacy is very important. I possibly
couldn’t stress enough how much. People will pay a premium amount of money to
have their own space and have thicker walls in an apartment building. A person
does not speak the same way in a space depending who is around. Why do we need
to dance alone in our rooms before dancing in front of a crowd? Why are ballots
usually secret? It’s important to be able to reflect in one’s own time too.
Privacy is an extension of self-ownership, to have the ability to choose how
much one shares about themselves, their thoughts and feelings. As it relate to
cryptocurrency, a privacy focused project such as Particl brings back privacy to
an increasingly public space, due to the power of technology and the current
need for people in the background to make things run smoothly as one uses their
product and services, such as services that help us manage our finances or
participate in the global marketplace.

PS: What excites you about Particl the most?

DRC: This isn’t something that I really knew before, but Particl was started by
a group of people who decided to work on something together, as opposed to an
already established institution funding a project. I think a great thing is when
people see a problem, and try to solve it, or have an idea, and get to work on
it. I know what Particl is doing in terms of implementing privacy technology is
cool, for lack of a better word, but I think it’s even cooler that people found
a way to put their skillsets together and create a community around an idea. I
also think in terms of seeing an issue with marketplaces such as how centralized
they are, or the lack of privacy in financial dealings in general as we rely on
banks, it is good to actually find a path independent of simply complaining
about how others are not doing it the right way.

PS: How did you get into doing video blogs?

DRC: I got into doing video blogs first as a way to share my artwork with
others, bring visibility to myself, and communicate with people who were fans of
my work. I then also began to share my voice with others on topics I thought
were important as I felt the social and political climate around me shifting.
Overtime, I found fulfillment in connecting with others through common
interests. I now appreciate the gift of being able to communicate with others in
a way that I did not before I was making video blogs.

PS: It is nice hearing someone explain they are still learning about technology
while they are also presenting it. Do you think you are just beginning,
somewhere in the middle or a certified blockchain expert regarding crypto?

DRC: I am definitely just beginning regarding crypto and understanding
blockchain technology! I could perhaps describe myself as an amateur. I have a
rudimentary understanding of cryptography. I think it will be insightful and
interesting for people to see my development and follow along as I learn.

Desi-Rae will continue building her new channel CryptoRamble
[https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCphRdyvqdktDRm0ruPqPRJA] on YouTube with
Particl deep-dives and educational pieces on blockchain solutions. While she’s
exclusive to Particl we also want to tap into the audience in the space still
learning.

We expect her to look deeper into blockchain trends like storage, platform
tokens, as well as, features that are Particl core values like privacy and
security.

Currency-agnosticism is also part of our DNA so don’t be alarmed to see Desi-rae
talking about other privacy coins or decentralized marketplaces. While she won’t
do full segments on specific coins (don’t even ask 😜) she will touch on
projects taking different approaches to this huge ecosystem just beginning to be
built.

She’s going to bring a valuable educational piece to our project and the space
and we are excited to support that. Together we all grow stronger!

You can catch Desi-Rae’s first new video on Particl here:

Thank You
Particl Team

node.plaintext:
We are proud to remove the “testnet” tag off our flagship application Particl
Desktop today and move it on to mainnet. Thank you to everyone testing it the
past 10 days as we ironed out the remaining issues.

As with all product releases, problems arise that we want to know about so we
can expedite a remedy as fast as possible.

Please continue reporting issues on the Particl Desktop repository here: 
https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/issues

New Features Overview
Multi-State Privacy Selection Modal
Within the Send window users will have the option to select their level of
privacy; Public, Blind or Anon.

 * Public = Pseudo-Anonymous
 * Blind = Confidential Transactions
 * Anon = Ring Confidential Transactions (RingCT)*

*Anon Transactions are currently being tested by researchers at NJIT. RingCT is
also available on Particl Testnet 6

Read More: https://particl.wiki/what-is#multi-state_privacy

Multi-State Privacy Balance Transfers
Similar to “Send Payment”, when using “Balance Transfer” users can send PART
between Public and Blind addresses only they own. Particl Desktop has an easy to
understand interface to lessen the learning curve on balance transfers.

Sort and Filter by Type
Within the History window Particl Desktop now has some handy filters to easily
find past transactions in your app.*

Sort transactions by:

 * Time
 * Amount
 * Address
 * Category
 * Confirmation

We are proud to remove the “testnet” tag off our flagship application Particl Desktop today and move it on to mainnet. Thank you to everyone testing it the past 10 days as we ironed out the remaining issues.

As with all product releases, problems arise that we want to know about so we can expedite a remedy as fast as possible.

Please continue reporting issues on the Particl Desktop repository here: https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/issues

New Features Overview

Multi-State Privacy Selection Modal

Within the Send window users will have the option to select their level of privacy; Public, Blind or Anon.

  • Public = Pseudo-Anonymous
  • Blind = Confidential Transactions
  • Anon = Ring Confidential Transactions (RingCT)*

*Anon Transactions are currently being tested by researchers at NJIT. RingCT is also available on Particl Testnet 6

Read More: https://particl.wiki/what-is#multi-state_privacy

Multi-State Privacy Balance Transfers

Similar to “Send Payment”, when using “Balance Transfer” users can send PART between Public and Blind addresses only they own. Particl Desktop has an easy to understand interface to lessen the learning curve on balance transfers.

Within the History window Particl Desktop now has some handy filters to easily find past transactions in your app.*

  • Time

* Take note that the latter will be less private. If Privacy is a high concern for you we would suggest allowing the cold staking process the needed time to stake the funds as intended.

node.plaintext:
Today we are unveil Particl Desktop; a new name for our powerful, open-source
Particl Wallet application built on Electron [https://electronjs.org/] and
Angular.

These are the same tools popular apps like Github, Slack, Discord, Signal, Riot
and Skype are built on!

Particl Desktop
Particl Wallet, or Particl GUI, worked good enough as a name for the task it
provided; Be a Wallet.

But, as we merge in new features — features beyond simply moving currency — we
needed a name that reflected the function of our flagship application.

> Particl is more than a privacy coin, it’s a privacy platform.
The new Particl Desktop app will be our same, very popular wallet everyone has
been so high on since it was released, plus more. Everyone is anticipating the
utility features coming out this quarter like the E2EE communication tools and
decentralized marketplace.

Particl Desktop is the app all those cool things get rolled into.

Particl Desktop 1.1.0-RC4 (testnet)
Particl Desktop releases exclusively on Testnet today. This build fixes a lot of
bugs and also has some new, cool features like ZAP. We need as many testers
volunteering to find issues/bugs with this release candidate (RC4) before we
make this available on Mainnet in 10 days.

particl/particl-desktop [https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases]
particl-desktop - Particl Angular GUI - The source for the Particl GUI.
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases]github.com
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/releases]

Glossary
> Mainnet- Particl’s live blockchain. This is the chain we all use to send PART 
between addresses.- Explorer: https://explorer.particl.io/
Testnet- Particl’s testing environment. This is a separate blockchain from
Mainnet with separate coins from Mainnet.
- Explorer: https://explorer-testnet.particl.io/

Testnet PART won’t show up on the Mainnet blockchain and vice-versa for Mainnet
PART and Testnet blockchain.

Release Notes
The team spent time fixing bugs, adding new features and streamlining the flow
of the GUI. Some pages have been updated to better the user experience.
Furthermore, a major restructure effort over the GUI codebase has been
completed.

The code is now more modular, less prone to errors and takes advantage of the
capabilities that the latest Angular provides. This pre-marketplace code
restructuration will enable us to implement features like auto-update and
multiwallet in a much better way.

As the marketplace will also be integrated soon, we renamed the software to the
simple and beautiful “Particl Desktop”. This software is intended to include the
wallet, the marketplace, secure chat & more. You can now find it under the 
particl-desktop [https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop] repository of the 
Particl GitHub organization [https://github.com/particl].

Test the Particl Desktop to discover all the other improvements we made, and do
not hesitate to point out things we might have overlooked in our inevitable
humanity (see below to open issues).

ZAP
The addition of the ZAP functionality will greatly assist in successful cold
staking activation*. This feature brings the option to:

 * Force (ZAP) that last few PART over to the cold stake address that might take
   a long time to stake otherwise
 * Force (ZAP) funds from small wallets to the cold staking address
 * Quickly activate cold staking, by means of sending all funds to the cold
   staking address in a single transaction
 * Take note that the later will be less private. If Privacy is a high concern
   for you we would suggest allowing the cold staking process the needed time to
   stake the funds as intended.

Features
 * Cold staking widget
 * ZAP functionality for fast cold staking activation
 * Debug window console
 * History filters
 * Send page redesign
 * Syncing progress bar in left side menu
 * Notifications for a new release
 * OS notifications for stakes and incoming transactions
 * Display client and daemon versions
 * Paste button in address add
 * Paste an address anywhere in send page and address book
 * Display public narrations on transactions
 * Choose how many transactions to display per page
 * Added background image for Mac DMG
 * Contributing guidelines
 * Upgrade dependencies
 * Upgraded partilcd to latest 0.15.1.2

Bugs Fixed
A BIG thanks to the community members who helped document the bugs and provided
error logs.

 * Balance transfer blind -> public
 * Blank modals
 * Diverse length seeds importing
 * Confirmations updating
 * Mac tray icon
 * Daemon messages
 * Daemon restart (encrypting wallet)
 * Auto locking wallet
 * Blind balance display
 * App not quitting when daemon start before the GUI
 * Too much RPC calls to the daemon
 * Fixed preceding issue on creation of wallet, due to passwords.
 * Sign and Verify modal refactoring
 * Changed save button name on address book

Known errors
The Mac build has a known graphical bug reported here
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/issues/753]. This should be fixed in
Electron 1.8.2 release captured in the comment here
[https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/pull/758#issuecomment-360430998].

Community Testers and Developers
Please open issues for every bug that is not already documented here*:

 * https://github.com/particl/particl-desktop/issues

*We will do our best to fix them before 1.1.0 official mainnet release, or plan
them for next releases.

Our open discussion channels for testing are:
 * Riot [https://riot.im/app/#/room/#particl-testing:matrix.org]
 * Slack [http://slack.particl.io/]: #testing channel
 * Discord [https://discord.gg/YWCjFvM]

Particl Testnet Faucet
Get free testnet PART here:

 * http://faucet.particl.xyz/

Particl Desktop v1.1.0 — Mainnet Release
Get ready !

Particl Desktop v1.1.0 will be available on Mainnet 10 days from today.

This will give the community testers and developers time to find and fix issues
on testnet before going live on the main chain.

We’re excited with the new name and clarity a single flagship application
represents as the jumping off point for joining the Particl privacy platform
network.

Thank you,
Particl Team

node.plaintext:
Today we introduce a third proposal type to our suite of proposals available to
everyone for the purpose of directly impacting the development, marketing and
adoption of the Particl (PART) protocol.

Particl Request For Proposals (RFP)
A Particl RFP is a document intended to solicit proposals for a task such as the
development of a community resource (ie: website), or creation of content pieces
not specifically identified in Particl Opportunities
[https://github.com/particlcommunity/pops].

particlcommunity/rfps [https://github.com/particlcommunity/rfps]
rfps - Particl Request For Proposals (RFP)
[https://github.com/particlcommunity/rfps]github.com
[https://github.com/particlcommunity/rfps]

 * RFP Template
   [https://github.com/particlcommunity/rfps/blob/master/rfp-0000/rfp-0000.mediawiki]

RFP 1 | Particl Cold Staking Pool
Create a cold staking pool for users to delegate their staking power to a pool
operator in exchange for smaller payouts. Once completed, the code shall be
open-sourced for anyone to inspect/audit.

What is Cold Staking?
Cold staking is enabled by smart-contract functionality and lets users securely
delegate staking powers to “staking nodes” which contain no coin. The purpose of
these “staking nodes” is to provide a dedicated resource connected to the
Particl blockchain and stake on behalf of another wallet without being able to
spend its coins.

Cold staking nodes are intended to be used in combination with cold, hardware
and paper wallets as well as multisig addresses, making it possible to stake
“offline” coins with no risk of being hacked or exposing your public key to the
network. Staking nodes can be set up on any device, secure or not, such as
public/cloud servers, virtual machines or RPIs.

Scope
The recent cold staking hardfork has enabled the possibility to set up cold
staking pools where operators cannot run with their users’ coins. The recent
cold staking hardfork has enabled the possibility to set up cold staking pools
where operators cannot run with their users’ coins. This request for proposal
(RFP) is to solicit proposals from qualified sources to create a Particl Staking
Pool.

Milestones*
 1. Setup a cold staking pool
 2. Setup a website for users to interact with the pool
 3. Make the code open-source

*For more information visit the RFP
[https://github.com/particlcommunity/rfps/blob/master/rfp-0001/rfp-0001.mediawiki]
.

Completion Reward
 * Base Reward: 250 PART
 * Contribution Address: Ps41Lwrc9jpxpwyXhMgroZQ2SQzytR62nh

We are excited about these proposal initiatives and our hope is it not only
encourages community development on the open market but grows the Particl (PART)
community development base by rewarding contributors for their hard work!

Thank You
Particl Team

node.plaintext:
I’m happy to announce the winners of our Community Bounty Contest to create an
updated Particl informational page at BitcoinWiki.org
[https://en.bitcoinwiki.org/wiki/Main_Page]

BitcoinWiki.org is:

> an independent project existing thanks to the active members of the
cryptocurrency community.
Bitcoin Wiki [https://en.bitcoinwiki.org/wiki/Main_Page]
The first blockchain was conceptualised in 2008 by an anonymous person or group
known as Satoshi Nakamoto and… [https://en.bitcoinwiki.org/wiki/Main_Page]
en.bitcoinwiki.org [https://en.bitcoinwiki.org/wiki/Main_Page]

It boasts 3,604 articles in English at the time of this announcement.
Impressive!

A Request For Help
Our Telegram channel [https://t.me/particlproject] got a request from “Max” at
Bitcoin Wiki

It seemed like a great opportunity for volunteers to lend a hand so we quickly
came up with a rough outline and threw in some PART as an incentive to
contribute!

Bounty Winners
 * *CryptoGuard— 50 PART
 * Part-linux— 25 PART
 * Serendipity— 10 PART
 * Kapoorsudartha— 10 PART
 * BB2k18–10 PART
 * Fragile— 7.5 PART

I have to say, I was impressed with the teamwork you guys showed breaking down
all the details of the Project into a Wiki format!

This page will be a beacon of information regarding Particl and it was all
community developed and delivered.
Bravo!

> *FYI: This work was done before CryptoGuard joined
[https://particl.news/longtime-contributor-cryptoguard-joins-particl-as-lead-community-officer-f33d87ef18df] 
the team.
Breakdown of contributions
It was truly a team effort

 * Description/concept: Kapoorsudartha, Part-linux, CryptoGuard
 * Token exchange: CryptoGuard
 * CT / RingCT: CryptoGuard, Part-linux
 * Smart-contract BIP: Part-linux
 * Graphics MAD Escrow: CryptoGuard, Part-linux, BB2k18
 * Foundation: Kapoorsudartha, Serendipity, CryptoGuard
 * Links: Part-linux, Fragile
 * Market token distro: CryptoGuard
 * API: Fragile

I will be reaching out to each of you to get your PART receiving address and
send your bounty rewards.

My personal favorite exercise I saw
One of the hottest debates we always have when explaining Particl is “What is
Particl?”

 * Particl is a privacy currency
 * Particl is a privacy marketplace
 * Particl is a privacy platform.

CryptoGuard took the time to play each scenario out with our products, features
and future developments in these outlines:

Outline 1 is Particl as a Privacy PlatformThis exercise lead to a very easy to
follow Bitcoin Wiki page outline for Particl as a Privacy Platform. I think
anyone who is looking for more information about our project will surely find it
easy and logical to read.

Go, have a look for yourself
Particl - Bitcoin Wiki [https://en.bitcoinwiki.org/wiki/Particl]
Particl - An open source and decentralized platform
[https://en.bitcoinwiki.org/wiki/Particl]en.bitcoinwiki.org
[https://en.bitcoinwiki.org/wiki/Particl]

More opportunities to earn PART in bounties, contests, etc coming soon™️

Join us in our Riot/Telegram channels to learn about ways you can get involved:

 * Riot Channels: https://particl.wiki/socialmedia-channels#riot
 * Telegram Channels: https://particl.wiki/socialmedia-channels#telegram

Thank you again for your contributions guys!
Particl Team

node.plaintext:
Welcome Christina Migneault-Khoury and James Cressler!
Fresh off the announcement
[https://particl.news/longtime-contributor-cryptoguard-joins-particl-as-lead-community-officer-f33d87ef18df] 
of CryptoGuard joining our communications team as Lead Community Manager, I’m
excited to introduce two more additions to the Particl team you’ll be seeing a
lot of in our social media networks.

Christina Migneault-Khoury
Social Media Strategist
https://www.linkedin.com/in/christina-migneault-khoury-a0433884/Christina is
coming from outside the cryptocurrency space, but while fresh on the scene, she
is no rookie at the social media space. Most of her professional experience has
been focused on digital marketing, consumer science and customer retention
through social media. Her areas of expertise include managing social media
accounts and developing proper strategies to maximize user engagement and
content exposure.

Focus

 * Social Media Research & Management
 * Graphics/multimedia
 * Content Creation

Christina’s role within our community team is going to be crucial, especially as
the entire crypto scene is heavily turning towards social media platforms. Her
mission will be to:

Research:

She will perform a thorough research of the best social media marketing
strategies to adopt in the crypto scene. The goals are to increase/maximize the
number of real followers, increase their engagement and help our content get
more exposure on social media platforms. As social media and trends evolve
quickly, the “research” part of her job will stay an ongoing process for the
duration of her time working with us. Additionally, Christina will constantly be
keeping an eye on competitors’ social media accounts to detect any trend, topic
of interest, strategies, and etc.

Analysis:

Once deployed, Christina will empirically monitor the effectiveness of the
aforementioned strategies by analyzing the metrics they generate and then adjust
our game plan as data come in. She will also be keeping track of any trend,
popular post, or topic of interest within the crypto community so that we can
better craft our social media content and maximize our exposure.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

James Cressler
Social Media Coordinator
James has a background in Business Administration and has been involved in
crypto since 2010. I think he or Joe takes the prize on our team for longevity
in this space — the exception might be Shazzy who shares how he stumbled upon
Bitcoin in 2009/2010 but wasn’t interested… :

James operates a family owned nursery business in “middle America” which I find
fascinating. Not the fact that his location is rural US, but that the same
obstacles of adoption and awareness and loyalty our Particl project encounters
in a crowded sea of noise, James deals with everyday.

He’s an example of the type of vendor that might come into Particl Marketplace
to create more opportunities for his products to be seen and sold. He also is in
a constant war of attrition with larger businesses dropping a mega store in the
middle of his territory; offering more options at lower prices helped by sheer
volume.

Focus

 * Facebook/Twitter
 * graphics
 * content creation

Like most of us, James is a staunch advocate for privacy and basic human rights.
It’s the lure of personal freedom that made him passionate about
cryptocurrencies and he recognizes it as a core value of Particl that he will
wave the banner for on our social media platforms.

Management:

Both James and Christina will join with the management all of our social media
accounts and coordinate them together according to our social media strategy.

Content Creation:

Another area we wanted help with as we searched for talent was creating content.
James and Christina have skills with graphics tools such as Illustrator,
Photoshop, Adobe Premiere, and etc. This will be invaluable as we add more
infographics, images, videos and social media content to help people better
understand our project.

Community Involvement
Join us in our Riot/Telegram channels to learn about ways you can get involved:

 * Riot Channels: https://particl.wiki/socialmedia-channels#riot
 * Telegram Channels: https://particl.wiki/socialmedia-channels#telegram

Welcome to the big happy family Christina and James!
Particl Team

Focus

  • Facebook/Twitter

  • content creation

Like most of us, James is a staunch advocate for privacy and basic human rights. It’s the lure of personal freedom that made him passionate about cryptocurrencies and he recognizes it as a core value of Particl that he will wave the banner for on our social media platforms.

Both James and Christina will join with the management all of our social media accounts and coordinate them together according to our social media strategy.

Another area we wanted help with as we searched for talent was creating content. James and Christina have skills with graphics tools such as Illustrator, Photoshop, Adobe Premiere, and etc. This will be invaluable as we add more infographics, images, videos and social media content to help people better understand our project.

node.plaintext:
Track your earning stats from your staking node!
The Particl Development Team released the latest version of the Particl Core
wallet: Release 0.15.1.1
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.15.1.1] which now
includes earning stats from your staking node!

Our merge of the latest Bitcoin Core 0.15.1 was part of our last core update
[https://particl.news/release-particl-core-0-15-1-0-fbeddf20ae5]. This update
includes additional functionality in the Particl client and is considered a
minor release.

Updates
 * getcoldstakinginfo - new RPC command; displays cold staking stats for current
   wallet
 * filtertransactions - added date range filtering support and collation of
   output
 * -createdefaultmasterkey - start particld or particl-qt with this to generate
   a random HD seed if no wallet exists.

How does this affect you?
Particl has a handful of official wallet clients: Particl Wallet
[https://github.com/particl/partgui/releases], Particl Copay
[https://github.com/particl/copay/releases] and Particl Core (cli/qt) wallets.
All of them rely on Particl Core for the backend muscle and security. Lucky for
you, all of them call for the latest protocol automatically!

In short, if you are running Particl Wallet or Particl Copay, you don’t need to
do anything. The next time you open each app they will call up Particl Core
0.15.1.1 automatically. If you prefer the cli or qt clients you have a choice:
continue with version 0.15.0.2 or newer or upgrade (recommended) to 0.15.1.1.

particl/particl-core
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.15.1.1]
particl-core - Particl Core integration/staging tree
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.15.1.1]github.com
[https://github.com/particl/particl-core/releases/tag/v0.15.1.1]

Partyman 0.6 Update
Shazzy has also updated the partyman [https://github.com/dasource/partyman] 
script that makes setting up Cold Staking on Particl easy and fun.

dasource/partyman [https://github.com/dasource/partyman]
Contribute to partyman development by creating an account on GitHub.
[https://github.com/dasource/partyman]github.com
[https://github.com/dasource/partyman]

Community member b.b.2k16 [https://medium.com/@leungclj] created a couple quick
gifs showing how to update partyman to version 0.6 and update to latest core
within partyman.


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

How to update partyman to version 0.6:
 1. ./partyman status in the partyman directory will tell you to upgrade
 2. git pull will grab the latest partyman script from Github
 3. ./partyman status will now show you are running the latest


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

How to update to Particl Core 0.15.1.1 within partyman version 0.6:
 1. ./partyman update grabs the latest core 0.15.1.1
 2. enter y installs the latest core
 3. ./partyman status verifies partyman updated core


--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Track earning stats on your staking node:
./partyman stakingnode stats

Thank you
Particl Team

node.plaintext:
https://www.linkedin.com/in/lachlan-k/The Particl Project is excited to welcome
on our newest developer, Lachlan Kang to the team!
Bio
My broad expertise is in security, especially network security, with a focus on
online anonymity networks. This specialisation encompasses many things, from
tracking technologies such as browser fingerprinting to anonymity networks such
as Tor and I2P and the secrets within them such as darknet black markets.

Studies
Currently a PhD student at University of Adelaide in Australia, Lachlan’s
research focuses on defeating anonymity networks such as Tor through use of
tracking technologies such as browser fingerprinting, traffic analysis, and
website fingerprinting.

Projects
I run a browser fingerprinting website ( https://browserprint.info ) which I
personally built from scratch using Java and MariaDB (MySQL).

Passion
Almost all my work has involved anonymity, security, or networking, however I
also have a side interest in cryptocurrencies.

Background
I’ve been programming for about 10 years, since my teens, and have used a large
variety of languages from C++, to Lisp, to Prolog, and many others in the past.

My best language is Java, however I use Perl very often and I’m also an adept C
programmer. I grew up on Windows, however I’ve been using Linux almost
exclusively for the past 5 or 6 years and am loving it.

Responsibilities
Again, the team is excited to see what Lachlan brings to the team, the Particl
Project and the upcoming Particl Marketplace. His focus will be on the
Marketplace command line interface (cli).

Expect some of his passion for anonymity, security and networks to bleed into
other pieces of the Project 😉

Welcome Lachlan!
Particl Team
